figures.caption: Table 2: Performance of the 4 RDMDL versions and 17 other methods on the Tübingen benchmarks. The best results in each column are in bold; the second best is underlined.

Table 8: Performance of the sixteen implemented causal discovery methods, alongside the four versions of RDMDL, in three sets of synthetic benchmarks, evaluated using AUROC (accuracy). The value in bold corresponds to the best result in each column, and those underlined to the second best.

Table 4: Confusion matrix: in each cell, on the left, the result when taken by (L(X) − L(Y )) and on the right when (L(Y |X) − L(X|Y )) dominates. Green indicates a correct decision and red an incorrect decision. Top-left table: RDMDL-FD; top-right table: RDMDL-R; bottom-left table: RDMDL-Sc; bottom-right table: RDMDL-St.
Figure 1: AUDRC curves in the Tübingen benchmark. Left: RDMDL (green) compared to all other implemented methods (shades of red); right: analysed MDL causal discovery methods.
